summary: eth-wallet-sentinel ships wallet/system scrapers with hardcoded GitHub-Pages C2
details: The package contains multiple modules (wallet-scanner.js, scanner.js, build-verifier.js) that combine filesystem enumeration, OS host identity collection (os.hostname(), os.userInfo equivalents), shell execution (execSync, curl, whoami, ping), and outbound HTTPS to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js:161 and scanner.js:323, with corresponding https.request/https.get plumbing). The package name advertises an Ethereum wallet sentinel, and the wallet-scanner.js module performs filesystem walks (fs.existsSync over many paths) combined with hostname collection and POSTs to remote endpoints, matching the wallet/credential stealer fingerprint. build-verifier.js additionally executes 'docker push' via execSync, suggesting registry abuse / artifact staging. The combination of (a) hardcoded C2 on a GitHub Pages lookalike account with no relation to a declared publisher, (b) wallet-directory enumeration and host identity collection feeding HTTPS POSTs, and (c) shell-driven curl/ping/execSync orchestration constitutes an installer-side credential and wallet stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
